rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use implementation scopes for output assignments only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with proper scope placement.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions match the actual AADL data types of referenced features.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Use boolean helper declarations for range checks with proper type alignment.
Do not reference opaque Boolean data in numeric comparisons; omit unsafe clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope require existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed exposure.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
